---
author: "Martin Westgate"
title: "R packages for data quality"
subtitle: "What's already out there"
date: 2026-04-17
image: bdc_logo.png
categories:
  - packages
---

There are a bunch of R packages available for getting biodiversity data. It is worth saying, first of all, that the data aggregators themselves do a lot of work on data quality. GBIF and the living atlases have systems that tag (and sometimes, alter) records as having various problems. Therefore `galah` and `rgbif` are relevant resources here; but that's a topic for a different time. This page is focussed on R packages that have cleaning biodiversity data as their central purpose, or include a cleaning component as part of some wider workflow.


```{r read-packages}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(gt)

htmltools::strong("Table 1: R packages for data quality listed on CRAN")

file <- here::here("analysis", "data-quality-r-packages", "data", "r_packages.csv")
df <- readr::read_csv(file)

df |>
  filter(category == "cleaning", on_cran == TRUE) |>
  mutate(name_url = glue::glue("<a href={url_cran}>{name}</a>")) |>
  mutate(doi = glue::glue("<a href = https://doi.org/{publication_doi}>{publication_doi}</a>")) |>
  mutate(docs = glue::glue("<a href={url_docs}>{sub('^https://', '', url_docs)}</a>")) |>
  select(name_url, date_updated, docs, doi) |>
  gt() |>
  fmt_url(columns = "name_url",
          show_underline = "false") |>
  fmt_url(columns = "docs") |>
  fmt_url(columns = "doi") |>
  cols_align(align = "left") |>
  cols_label(name_url = "Package",
      date_updated = "Updated",
      docs = "Documentation",
      doi = "DOI")
```
